_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.

Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

must_detect.matched_text:
STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — NON-PUBLIC — Project Saffron Dune — EMBARGO until 20 May 2026 09:00 AST — RBE Listing Hold
Operational cyber incident report: On 22 Apr 2026 (14 Shawwal 1447 AH), Al Nuwair Energy CJSC detected credential stuffing on vendor-facing APIs hosted at the Al Qassim plant.
The attack caused a 3.6% OT sensor outage and deferred invoicing by SAR 12.4m, with a provisional Q2 revenue impact of 2.5% pending remediation.
PDPL data categories at risk: masked Iqama IDs (e.g., IQ: 2 1 • • • • • • 7 8 9), masked Commercial Registration (CR: 4 0 3 0 • • • • 9 0), and work emails of suppliers; lawful basis asserted under urgent security necessity.
A “SDAIYAH” transfer-portal ticket KX-2047 was opened to evaluate cross-border log analysis; no outbound transfer has been executed and logs remain stored in-Kingdom.
Issuer workflow note: Board memo prepared for RBE disclosure protocol and pre-brief to the CMAK Market Conduct team; IR draft Q&A v3 is attached for Chair review.
Deal context (MNPI): under Project Saffron Dune, a definitive SPA dated 18 May 2026 was executed with Desert Palms Industrial LLC for divestment of the plastics unit, price SAR 480.0m subject to working-capital true-up.
The SPA includes a MAE/MAC clause; current assessment is that the incident does not constitute, and is not reasonably likely to constitute, a MAC as defined in Section 8.2.
Term sheet variance for buyer financing: escrow raised by SAR 18.0m and earn-out reduced by 2.5%; do not discuss externally before the embargo window lapses.
Public note: the vendor’s 2024 uptime targets and our cyber policy summary are already available in last year’s sustainability report and local press briefings.
lowercase pm note — align with it and procurement; then ping hr about the desert spa-day vouchers; this is not material and should not go into any market communication.
Contact: Mr. Hatem Al-Mirfa, Board Secretary, sec@alnuwair.example.sa, +966-11-555-0142; operational lead: Eng. Reem Al-Atheer, reem.atheer@alnuwair.example.sa; DPO: Dr. Layla Al-Harqani, dpo@alnuwair.example.sa, +966-11-555-0199 ext 772.
O b f u s c a t e d I D s: C R 4 0 3 0**9 0; I Q 1 0**4 4 5 5; site code RYD-PLT-0 3; warehouse GPS h t t p : / / maps.internal.example/geo?lat=24.7&lon=46.7 (internal only).
Internal link: https://risk.rbe.example/rpt?case=PDPL-INC%2F2026-04-22&embargo=2026-05-20T09%3A00%2B03%3A00&codename=Saffron%20Dune.
Board minute: if remediation slips past 5 days, notify lenders per covenant 6.1, otherwise no change to guidance; research note draft remains under embargo and is not to be circulated.
